title: [InstCombine] Extend (icmp eq/ne (and Z, X), (and Z, Y)) folds
summary: The developers generalized the pattern by relaxing the `m_OneUse` constraint and adding a cost-benefit analysis to ensure transformation safety. Originally, the optimization required both `and` operations to have a single use. The generalization allows multiple uses but imposes new conditions: either (1) the xor of the compared values must be a negated power-of-two (enabling a later fold), or (2) at least two of the three key instructions (the two `and` ops or the xor) must be single-use or constant-foldable. This ensures the transformation is instruction-neutral or beneficial, avoiding unnecessary instruction duplication. The approach balances pattern coverage with profitability, extending the optimization to vectors and cases with extra uses while preserving performance. Developers can apply this strategy by identifying core patterns, then relaxing constraints incrementally with guardrails to maintain efficiency.
